title: Emergence of prethermal time quasicrystalline order in a quasiperiodically driven non-interacting spin chain
abstract: We study prethermal time quasicrystalline (TQC) order in a quasiperiodically driven chain of non-interacting spin-1/2 particles. The drive consists of two parts, switched on and off periodically with frequency $ω_d$: (i) disordered Ising interactions, with exchange couplings chosen from a symmetric interval $[-J/2, J/2]$, allowing random antiferromagnetic or ferromagnetic nearest-neighbor couplings, together with a random transverse field; and (ii) a rotating transverse magnetic field with frequency $Ω$.   The ratio $ω_d/Ω$ is chosen to be irrational, producing multiple incommensurate frequencies and yielding quasiperiodic dynamics beyond Floquet theory. Using exact diagonalization, we analyze the time autocorrelation function, dynamical structure factor, and entanglement entropy (EE). In the high-frequency regime, robust spectral peaks at incommensurate frequencies (not integer multiples of the fundamental drives) signal quasiperiodic time-translation symmetry breaking (QTTSB). The EE exhibits sublinear power-law growth followed by a prethermal plateau, indicating suppressed resonant heating due to an energy scale mismatch. The nonequilibrium lifetime increases rapidly with driving frequency. Unlike symmetric disorder sampling, an asymmetric distribution of the Ising exchange couplings induces collective spin rigidity, enhancing the system's resistance to heating. The TQC phase remains stable against next-nearest-neighbor (NNN) exchange perturbations and rotational imperfections, with robustness comparable to discrete time crystals (TCs) under periodic driving. Our results establish this quasiperiodically driven system as a platform for long-lived nonequilibrium temporal order, revealing the interplay of disorder, collective rigidity, and quasiperiodic driving.
summary: This paper demonstrates that a spin chain driven by two incommensurate frequencies can exhibit a stable, prethermal time quasicrystalline phase. The researchers show that this phase features quasiperiodic time-translation symmetry breaking and is resistant to heating. The findings highlight how the interplay of disorder and quasiperiodic driving can create long-lived nonequilibrium states.
main_problem: The study investigates the emergence and stability of prethermal time quasicrystalline (TQC) order in a spin chain subjected to quasiperiodic driving.
main_result: The authors demonstrate that quasiperiodic driving leads to quasiperiodic time-translation symmetry breaking (QTTSB) with robust spectral peaks at incommensurate frequencies and a suppressed heating regime characterized by a prethermal entanglement entropy plateau.
method: The study employs exact diagonalization to analyze the dynamics of the spin chain.
model_or_system: A non-interacting spin-1/2 chain with disordered Ising interactions (randomly chosen from a symmetric interval) and a random transverse field, driven by a two-part periodic process involving a rotating transverse magnetic field at an incommensurate frequency ratio.
key_observables: Time autocorrelation function, dynamical structure factor, and entanglement entropy (EE).
important_parameters: Driving frequencies (omega_d and Omega), the irrational ratio omega_d/Omega, the high-frequency regime, and the asymmetry of the Ising exchange coupling distribution.
main_assumptions: The system is treated as a non-interacting spin chain; the study focuses on the high-frequency regime to observe prethermal effects.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the quasiperiodically driven model, describes the numerical methodology using exact diagonalization, presents results for spectral features and entanglement entropy, analyzes the effects of disorder asymmetry and perturbations, and concludes with the implications for nonequilibrium temporal order.
why_interesting: It explores the intersection of quasiperiodic driving and disorder to create a new type of temporal order (TQC) that extends beyond standard Floquet-based discrete time crystals, offering insights into many-body dynamics and heating suppression.
analysis_basis: abstract only; PDF text extraction failed or PDF unavailable
